title: Pioneering AI researcher to advise RBC's machine learning lab | CBC News
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > 'The father of reinforcement learning'
Content:
For over three decades, Sutton has specialized in reinforcement learning. In this branch of machine learning, an algorithm is designed to receive either a reward or penalty based on its behaviour, and learns to make choices that will result in the most reward — and, hopefully, most desired behaviour — over time.
**[ANALYSIS | Scientists must act now to make artificial intelligence benign](http://www.cbc.ca/news/business/scientists-must-act-now-to-make-artificial-intelligence-benign-don-pittis-1.3182946)**
**[First-time dad applies machine learning to his newborn twins](http://www.cbc.ca/radio/spark/338-quantified-babies-botnet-boutiques-and-more-1.3886079/first-time-dad-applies-machine-learning-to-his-newborn-twins-1.3886088)**
That behaviour could be anything from a self-driving car successfully navigating a busy highway without incident, to a computer that most sends elevators most efficiently from floor to floor.

title: RBC Research engages top machine learning experts to bring reinforcement learning to the forefront of banking - RBC News & Stories
excerpts:
Section Title: RBC Research engages top machine learning experts to bring reinforcement learning to the forefron...
Content:
Loading
TORONTO , Jan. 18, 2017 - Following recent investments in artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning, RBC today announced Dr. Richard S. Sutton , one of the modern day pioneers of AI, as head academic advisor to RBC Research in machine learning. RBC Research will establish a new lab and plan to work with the Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute (Amii), based at the University of Alberta , to identify and pursue further research collaboration opportunities on an ongoing basis.
"We are thrilled to be opening a lab in Edmonton and to collaborate with world-class scientists like Dr. Sutton and the other researchers at Amii," said Dr. Foteini Agrafioti , head of RBC Research. "RBC Research has built strong capabilities in deep-learning, and with this expansion, we are well poised to play a major role in advancing research in AI and impact the future o

title: U of A professor wins Turing Prize for groundbreaking AI ...
excerpts:
Article content
“We wanted to be ambitious without being arrogant,” Sutton said. “Now it’s obvious that reinforcement learning is a thing to do, but it did not used to exist as a machine-learning endeavour.”
Article content
Sutton spoke about the award’s namesake, Turing, who once said, “What we want is a machine that learns from experience.” Despite all the progress made in the field, Sutton said, they still have not achieved that dream. Article content
He believes the future of AI won’t be in large language models but in systems that focus on reinforcement learning. Article content
“Still today, I’d say the importance of learning from experience is not appreciated,” Sutton said. “I’ve had to explain to so many people that learning from experience is not what modern AI does. It learns from people, and it tries to mimic people.”

title: The man who taught AI to learn believes human-level intelligence is ...
excerpts:
Section Title: The man who taught AI to learn believes human-level intelligence is closer than you think > Author
Content:
[Sascha Brodsky](https://www.ibm.com/think/author/sascha-brodsky)
Staff Writer
IBM
Richard Sutton, one of the pioneers behind modern artificial intelligence, is not convinced that simply throwing more computing power at AI will lead to machines that think like humans. In fact, he argues today’s obsession with scaling up deep learning might be holding AI back from its full potential.
Sutton, alongside his longtime collaborator Andrew Barto, [won this year’s Turing Award](https://amturing.acm.org/byyear.cfm) —often called the "Nobel Prize of Computing"—for his work in [reinforcement learning](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/reinforcement-learning) . He believes the real breakthrough will come when AI stops relying on curated datasets and starts learning from experience, much like a child does.
